data.title: Azerion Group publishes its 2025 Annual Report
data.description: Azerion Group N.V. (EURONEXT: AZRN) released its official 2025 Annual Report on April 17, 2026. The report, now available on www.azerion.com/reports/, has been submitted to the Dutch Authority for the Financial Markets (AFM) in PDF, ESEF, and HTML formats. The figures in the audited report align with the previously disclosed full-year 2025 results from February 26, 2026, showing revenues of €540.6 million and an adjusted EBITDA of €67.1 million for continuing operations. For more details, the complete story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: These Analysts Slash Their Forecasts On ManpowerGroup After Q1 Results
data.description: ManpowerGroup (NYSE: MAN) reported strong earnings for the first quarter, surpassing expectations. Quarterly earnings stood at 51 cents per share, beating the analyst consensus of 49 cents per share. The company's quarterly sales reached $4.510 billion, exceeding the estimated $4.414 billion. Looking ahead, ManpowerGroup anticipates second-quarter GAAP EPS between 91 cents and $1.05, falling short of market estimates of 96 cents. Jonas Prising, Chair & CEO of ManpowerGroup, expressed satisfaction with their performance, attributing it to prudent execution and stabilized demand in key markets. This marked the fifth consecutive quarter with an improvement in year-over-year revenue trends. The company expanded its pipeline, observed positive momentum in the Manpower brand, and increased operational efficiency through reductions in SG&A costs.

data.title: Citizens Financial Analysts Raise Their Forecasts After Better-Than-Expected Q1 Earnings
data.description: Citizens Financial Group (NYSE: CFG) exceeded expectations in their first quarter earnings report. They reported quarterly earnings of $1.13 per share, surpassing the analyst consensus of $1.09 per share. Quarterly sales were also higher than anticipated, reaching $2.168 billion compared to the expected $2.163 billion. Chairman and CEO Bruce Van Saun expressed satisfaction with the strong start in 2026 despite global tensions, citing a 47% year-over-year EPS growth, 7% positive operating leverage, and a 7 bps NIM expansion sequentially and 24 bps compared to the previous year. The company is in a robust financial position.

data.title: Bitcoin Hits $78,000 As 46 Days Of Short Positions Face Potential Short Squeeze
data.description: Bitcoin has surged by 5% after surpassing the descending trendline that had marked the bear trend from $128,000, triggering short-squeeze conditions. The current short squeeze is reminiscent of the 46-day streak that occurred between November and December 2022 surrounding the FTX collapse. Analysts, like Vetle Lunde, the head of research at K33, have noted that traders building short positions and betting against a breakout could lead to a short squeeze if upward momentum continues. If prices continue to rise, traders who went against the rally could face substantial losses, compelling them to repurchase positions simultaneously, resulting in a sharp uptick in prices due to a cascading short squeeze. The prolonged standstill intensifies the potential for an unwinding effect. Furthermore, Goldman Sachs recently made a significant move by applying for a Bitcoin exchange-traded fund (ETF), marking its first direct involvement in the crypto space. For the comprehensive story, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Moai Launch Party at John W. North Park with Blue Zones Project Riverside
data.description: Riverside, California witnesses a community celebration as Blue Zones Project Riverside organizes a Moai™ Launch Party at North Park on April 18th, 2026, from 10:00 AM to 12:00 PM. The event aims to bring Riversiders together for a common purpose, with over 300 individuals expected to attend.

data.title: BONK set for 20% drop as technical signals weaken. RSI at 61.68; MACD momentum dead. Price likely at $0.000028 in two weeks.
data.description: The upward trend for BONK has come to an end as its Relative Strength Index (RSI) stands at 61.68, indicating a weakening momentum. The MACD also suggests a lack of buying strength. These factors point towards a potential drop to $0.000028 in the next two weeks, a significant decline from the current levels. This correction is expected due to overextended bullish activity in the market.

data.title: These Analysts Revise Their Forecasts On U.S. Bancorp After Q1 Results
data.description: U.S. Bancorp (NYSE: USB) released strong first-quarter 2026 results, exceeding expectations. The bank reported adjusted earnings per share of $1.18, surpassing the analyst consensus of $1.14. Quarterly sales reached $7.288 billion, beating the expected $7.277 billion. CEO Gunjan Kedia credited robust revenue growth for a positive operating leverage of 440 basis points, with investments and cost savings contributing to a 260 basis points improvement in efficiency ratio year-over-year. For the second quarter, U.S. Bancorp foresees a 6%-7% growth in net interest income and fees. Additionally, the bank anticipates a full-year revenue growth of 4%-6% with over 200 basis points of operating leverage. Following the report, U.S. Bancorp shares climbed by 3.4%.
